evidence.scientific_clarity: Sankey diagram directly addresses the brief's dataset-prep pipeline; proportional flows and node labels make the 1120→1000→820→(600/120/100) story immediately readable.
evidence.typography: Arial explicitly set (family='Arial', sizes 13/16) — no DejaVu Sans default — but pdf.fonttype=42 is a matplotlib concept; Plotly/kaleido rasterizes output so text is not editable in the PDF.
evidence.palette: Green (#55A868) for Cleaned and red (#C44E52) for Filtered are used together — classic deuteranopia/protanopia failure; these two nodes are indistinguishable for ~8% of male readers.
evidence.layout_density: 820×480 canvas with 30 px side margins and 60 px top margin; seven-node Sankey fills the space adequately with no obvious gaping whitespace or label collisions.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption enumerates all counts and explains flow-width encoding and link-color inheritance; node labels double as legend; no separate legend box needed, but the dual 'Reject' sink (120+60) could confuse without clarification.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains 28 embedded raster /Subtype /Image objects and zero BT/ET text operators or path operators — kaleido writes a raster-in-PDF, not a true vector file; text is not selectable.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (70 KB), figure.png (129 KB, 1640×960), caption.tex (384 B), gen_figure.py (2.3 KB) all present and non-trivial; bonus figure.svg also generated.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states specific counts at every stage (1120, 1000, 120 rejected, 820, 60 rejected, 600/120/100 split), explains visual encoding (flow widths proportional, link colours from source), and matches the figure exactly.

overall_strengths: All required output files are present and non-trivial; the SVG bonus adds a genuinely selectable-text format.
Caption is numerically precise and self-contained, explaining both the data and the visual encoding conventions.
overall_weaknesses: Plotly/kaleido produces a raster-in-PDF (28 embedded PNG tiles, no vector paths or selectable text), failing the vector-fidelity requirement despite the .pdf extension.
Green+red node colors (Cleaned/Filtered) are colorblind-unsafe for deuteranopia/protanopia; a blue/orange or sequential palette would be safer.
